page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across several standard workloads. The instance achieves average ratings in Geekbench single-core (1315.0) and multi-core (5025.0) tests, as well as in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. Cryptographic operations perform steadily, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets. However, raw computational throughput shows limitations, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests scoring in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Memory bandwidth is average for cached small-block reads (395.92 GB/sec) but drops to weak levels for uncached large-block operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c6a.2xlarge offers a dedicated hardware profile suitable for entry-level compute-bound tasks without the overhead of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. Its balanced performance in static web serving (6.78 GB/sec) and Redis SET operations (7.44 million operations per second) makes it a viable option for web serving, lightweight database operations, and general-purpose utility workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand extreme memory bandwidth or high-performance computing capabilities.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, software compilation, and static web serving, though raw CPU stress tests and uncached memory bandwidth fall into lower percentiles. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for dedicated compute workloads that do not require local storage. It is best suited for web serving, Redis databases, and moderate computational tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average performance for web serving and database tasks.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB RAM, and average web-serving performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute instance offering balanced memory bandwidth and steady performance for web serving and database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on KVM hypervisor
Average performance in static web serving and Redis operations
Suitable for web servers, software compilation, and moderate databases
